The North African country has been described as the world's largest humanitarian challenge, notably in terms of displacement and hunger.

title: 168 children killed by Israel's strikes in Lebanon
description: Jawad Younes, 11, and his cousins were playing soccer in the lot between their houses, as they often did. Minutes later, an Israeli strike came. Jawad and his cousin are among 168 children killed — of more than 2,100 people in all — by Israel's strikes in the six weeks of renewed war between the country and Iran-backed Hezbollah.

title: Israeli army issues new evacuation call to south Lebanon residents
description: Israel's military on Wednesday issued a fresh evacuation order for residents of south Lebanon as it pressed on with an offensive against Hezbollah militants. It is the first evacuation order by the military since direct talks were held on Tuesday in Washington between Lebanon and Israel's ambassadors to the United States. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

title: After three years of war, exiled Sudanese find their place in Kenya
description: This Wednesday marks three years since the start of the civil war in Sudan, a conflict that has forced 14 million people to flee their homes. Some 4 million of them have left their country altogether. While many have fled to neighbouring countries, some have chosen Kenya. The capital Nairobi has become a hub for welcoming not only Sudanese refugees, but also political and even armed groups linked to the conflict.

title: US urges IMF, World Bank to abandon climate finance goals
description: The climate-denialist Trump administration is pushing the World Bank and International Monetary Fund to stop focusing on clean energy development projects. We take a closer look. Next, war robots in Ukraine have captured Russian positions for the first time in history. Meanwhile, the war in Sudan enters its fourth year. Elsewhere, the World Press Photo awards have been given out. Finally, an unusual campaign takes the UK by storm.

title: Toddler among 10 killed in Israeli strikes on Gaza
description: Gaza's civil defence agency said on Tuesday that a toddler was among 10 people killed in separate Israeli strikes in the northern part of the Palestinian territory. Violence continues despite a ceasefire in the Gaza war that came into effect on October 10, with both Israel and Hamas regularly accusing each other of violations.

title: Spain launches programme to offer amnesty to 500,000 undocumented migrants
description: As countries on both sides of the Atlantic ramp up deportations of undocumented migrants, Spain’s left-wing government on Tuesday prepared to give legal status to hundreds of thousands of irregular workers. Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez has championed the amnesty as a way to not only give informal workers legal protections, but to also bring more money into a social security system increasingly under stress by the country's ageing population.

title: Pope to urge peace in Cameroon's conflict zone
description: Pope Leo XIV is to arrive in Cameroon on Wednesday, the second stage of an African tour shadowed by insults from US President Donald Trump and suicide attacks in Algeria on his first day. The pontiff is to meet Cameroonian President Paul Biya at the start of his four-day visit to the mainly French-speaking country, before heading to a conflict zone where English-speaking separatists have been fighting the army for nearly a decade.

title: Paris Saint-Germain beats Liverpool to reach the Champions League semifinals
description: Paris Saint-Germain’s grip on the Champions League trophy remains strong. The defending champion advanced to the semifinals of European club soccer’s biggest competition by dumping out six-time winner Liverpool on Tuesday. A 2-0 win at Anfield sealed a 4-0 aggregate victory and moved PSG a step closer to becoming only the second club to retain the trophy in the modern era after all-time king of Europe Real Madrid.

title: Frenchman scoops up 1 million euro Picasso painting in Paris raffle
description: A ⁠Frenchman won ​a Picasso painting worth 1 million euros ($1.18 million) in a non-profit raffle on Tuesday. The ​winner, Ari Hodara, a 59-year-old software engineer from Paris, was selected at random at Christie's auction house in Paris from 120,000 tickets sold at 100 euros apiece. ​The raffle's proceeds ‌will fund Alzheimer's disease research.

title: France and UK to co-host Hormuz shipping security talks

President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer are to co-chair the meeting to discuss a plan to "restore freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz when security conditions allow it", it said.

description: Fighting continued in southern Lebanon between Israel and Iranian-backed Hezbollah militants, even as Israeli and Lebanese diplomats met in Washington. The war in Lebanon has threatened to undermine the U.S.-Iran cease-fire.

title: Полиция арестовала у гольф-клуба Трампа тяжеловооруженного мужчину
description: Полиция арестовала и предъявила обвинения американцу Шону Штайнеру, который прогуливался по элитному району Лос-Анджелеса недалеко от гольф-клуба президента Дональда Трампа, будучи до зубов вооруженным разным огнестрельным оружием, сообщает New York Post со ссылкой на полицию."Житель Глендейла Шон Штайнер, вооруженный до зубов, обвиняется в совершении нескольких тяжких преступлений, связанных с незаконным оборотом огнестрельного оружия, после тог

title: Ben and Jerry’s founder wants Magnum to sell brand; conglomerate says it’s not for sale
description:
In latest broadside against ice cream maker's parent company, Ben Cohen says it's stifling social activism
The post Ben and Jerrys founder wants Magnum to sell brand; conglomerate says its not for sale appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: College that pioneered Holocaust studies and birthed Yiddish Book Center to close
description:
Hampshire College cites declining enrollment, mounting debt as reasons it will shutter at the end of 2026 after 61 years; closure will not affect Yiddish Book Center
The post College that pioneered Holocaust studies and birthed Yiddish Book Center to close appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Israeli legal advocay group files ICC case against Spanish PM over exports to Iran
description:
Shurat HaDin accuses Pedro Sanchez's government of providing 'components required by the regime in Tehran and its proxies for military purposes'
The post Israeli legal advocay group files ICC case against Spanish PM over exports to Iran appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: CENTCOM says blockade of Iran ‘fully implemented,’ declares US ‘maritime superiority’ in Mideast
description:
Pakistani finance chief says his country will ‘not give up’ on efforts to mediate US-Iran negotiations * Trump says not thinking about extending Iran ceasefire, predicts ‘an amazing two days ahead’

title: U.S. Air Force Releases First Official Photos Of B-21 Raider During Aerial Refueling Tests
description: The USAF has just released the first image showing the upper side of the new B-21 Raider bomber in photos from its aerial refueling tests. On Apr. 14, 2026, the U.S. Air Force publicly acknowledged that the B-21 Raider has recently conducted aerial refueling from a KC-135 as part of its ongoing test and evaluation []

title: Champions League: PSG through to semis after defeating Liverpool
description: Paris Saint-Germain beat Liverpool (2-0) thanks to a brace from Ousmane Dembélé and have reached the semi-finals for the third year running.

title: Israeli strikes kill 10 in northern Gaza, including toddler, civil defence says
description: Gaza’s civil defence agency said on Tuesday that 10 people, including a toddler, were killed in separate Israeli strikes in the north of the enclave. Violence has persisted despite an October 10 ceasefire. A strike on a police vehicle in Gaza City killed four people, including a child, and wounded others.

title: Iran war live: Trump says war ‘close to over’ as Hormuz blockade continues
description: US president says second round of talks could be held in Pakistan as naval blockade of Iranian ports continues.

title: Watch: Vance heckled as he chides Pope
description: The US Vice-President was speaking at a Turning Point USA event, where he said Pope Leo should "be careful when he talks about matters of theology."

title: Man wins €1m Picasso painting in €100 charity raffle
description: "How do I know this isn't a prank?" the winner asked when he answered a video call from Christie's auction house in Paris.

title: South Africa appoints former apartheid-era negotiator as US ambassador
description: Roelf Meyer will replace the South African ambassador who was expelled from the US by President Donald Trump in 2025.
